input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS → iOS + Android.

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- **Version:** Install the latest stable release. MAUI's release notes specify the minimum supported Xcode version per .NET release — check [github.com/dotnet/maui/releases](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases).
- After install, run:
  ```
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  ```
- Accept the license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- Open Xcode → **Settings → Platforms** → download the iOS simulator runtime you need.

## 2. .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest stable SDK (e.g., .NET 9).
- Use the **macOS installer (`.pkg`)**. This includes the `dotnet` CLI.
- Verify: `dotnet --version`

## 3. .NET MAUI Workload

The MAUI workload installs MAUI templates, the Android SDK, and build tooling:

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- **Discover available versions:** `dotnet workload search maui`
- **See what's installed:** `dotnet workload list`
- **Update later:** `sudo dotnet workload update`

This single command pulls in the `android`, `ios`, `maui-android`, and `maui-ios` sub-workloads automatically.

## 4. Android Setup (handled mostly by the workload)

The MAUI workload installs a private Android SDK + NDK. However:

- **Java/OpenJDK:** The workload bundles Microsoft's OpenJDK build. If not present, install **Microsoft OpenJDK 17** from [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download).
- **Android emulator:** Create an AVD via command line:
  ```bash
  # Find the path the workload installed to:
  dotnet android sdk info

  # Or use the standalone Android command-line tools:
  sdkmanager "system-images;android-35;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
  avdmanager create avd -n Pixel_API_35 -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
  emulator -avd Pixel_API_35
  ```
- On Apple Silicon, use **arm64-v8a** images (x86_64 won't work).

## 5. (Optional) IDE

| Option | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) | Lightweight, free |
| **JetBrains Rider** | Full-featured, paid (with .NET MAUI support built-in) |

> *Visual Studio for Mac has been retired.* VS Code or Rider are the recommended options on macOS now.

## 6. Verify the Full Toolchain

```bash
# 1. Check SDK
dotnet --version

# 2. Check workloads
dotnet workload list
# Should show: maui, maui-android, maui-ios, android, ios

# 3. Create & build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# 4. Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run

# 5. Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

If all five commands succeed, your environment is fully working.

## Version Compatibility Cheat Sheet

| Check this | Where |
|---|---|
| Which Xcode version a .NET release needs | [github.com/dotnet/maui/releases](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) release notes |
| .NET SDK latest stable | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) |
| Workload / Android API level mapping | `dotnet workload search` + release notes |
| Known issues | [github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki/Known-Issues](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki/Known-Issues) |

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → create test project → build both targets.
